2008-oopsla-dtar.description: QUAIL introduces deep typechecking to ensure dynamic query safety and simplify refactoring in systems combining Java code with a database backend.

2009-pldi-pec.description: PEC automatically checks compiler optimization correctness by generalizing dynamic translation validation to verifying parameterized optimization templates.

2012-security-quark.description: Quark is a web browser kernel formally verified in Coq to ensure tab non-interference, cookie integrity, and address bar security via formal shim verification.

2019-conga-sinkingpoint.description: Sinking-point extends arbitrary digital number systems to dynamically track precision and help identify results with high error with few false positives.

2021-arith-herbie.description: Pareto Herbie optimizes floating-point code by interleaving precision tuning and rewriting to generate diverse accuracy vs. speed trade-offs.

2021-maps-glenside.description: Glenside is a pure IR for ML compilers that enables term rewriting by leveraging access patterns for HW mapping and discovering data layout transformations.

2021-oopsla-ruler.description: Ruler uses equality saturation and e-graphs to infer rewrite rules, leading to 25x faster synthesis of 5.8x smaller rulesets that match or exceed experts.

2021-sff-3dp.description: Project investigating multi-headed desktop printers for simultaneous parallel 3D printing, aiming to reduce fabrication time and enhance rapid prototyping.

2022-ecoop-cakemlfp.description: RealCake extends CakeML with verified fast-math-style optimizations, enabling end-to-end correctness by relating abstract real-number semantics to concrete I/O semantics.

2022-fmcad-smallproofs.description: A practical O(n log(n)) method for generating small proofs from congruence closure, plus a novel optimal O(n^5) algorithm. Implemented in egg, faster than Z3.

2022-popl-rematch.description: Relational e-matching uses DB query techniques to provide optimal e-matching, simple implementation, and orders-of-magnitude speedup vs. conventional methods.

2022-tog-carpentry.description: Joint optimization of carpentry designs & fabrication plans with e-graphs. Discovering Pareto-optimal tradeoffs to bridge design-to-fabrication gaps.

2023-oopsla-enumo.description: Enumo is a new DSL that makes EqSat rule inference programmable and scalable. Its core operators enable strategic guidance, supporting larger grammars and novel strategies previously out of scope.

2023-plarch-lakeroad.description: Generate Compilers from Hardware Models! - PLARCH @ PLDI 2023

2023-pldi-egglog.description: The new egglog fixpoint reasoning system fully combines Datalog and Equality Saturation to provide composable analyses and enable incremental term rewriting.

2023-popl-babble.description: LLMT, a new library learning algorithm implemented in our tool 'babble', efficiently identifies common patterns in code corpora via anti-unification to improve both compression and scalability.

2024-programming-magicmarkup.description: Magic Markup keeps document-external annotations aligned as text evolves using an LLM-backed re-tagging system.
